perturbation.original_text: "System Fuel & LUAF" shall mean, for any period of calculation, (i) Customer's share of Gas or fuel equivalents (electricity or other energy sources converted to their Btu equivalent) used by Transporter to deliver Customer's Gas from the Point(s) of Receipt to the Point(s) of Delivery on the Transportation System during such period, and (ii) Gas lost and unaccounted for on the Transportation System during such period.
perturbation.changed_text: "System Fuel & LUAF" shall mean, for any period of calculation, (i) Customer's share of Gas or fuel equivalents (electricity or other energy sources converted to their Btu equivalent) used by Pipeline Operator to deliver Customer's Gas from the Point(s) of Receipt to the Point(s) of Delivery on the Transportation System during such period, and (ii) Gas lost and unaccounted for on the Transportation System during such period.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Transporter' is changed to 'Pipeline Operator' in the definition of 'System Fuel & LUAF.' This creates confusion because the contract defines 'Transporter,' and it is the defined party responsible for operating the transportation system. Using 'Pipeline Operator' introduces ambiguity as it is unclear who is responsible for delivering the Customer's Gas, potentially contradicting the responsibilities assigned to the 'Transporter' throughout the rest of the agreement. This may cause confusion when 'System Fuel & LUAF' needs to be calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Article II. Dedication, Commencement Date Facilities, Post-Commencement Date Facilities and Transporter Services
perturbation.contradicted_text: For each Day during the Term, (i) Transporter agrees to provide Firm Service on the Transportation System for Customer Gas delivered to the Transportation System in a quantity not to exceed Customer's Firm Service Gas for such Day, and Interruptible Service for all quantities in excess of Customer's Firm Service Gas for such Day, and deliver to Customer, or for the account of Customer, at the applicable Points of Delivery, Equivalent Quantities of Customer Gas received at the Points of Receipt, and (ii) Customer, or its designee, shall accept such Equivalent Quantities of Customer Gas at the Points of Delivery.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Transporter' to 'Pipeline Operator' in the definition of 'System Fuel & LUAF' directly contradicts the section stating 'Transporter agrees to provide Firm Service.' The contract previously assigned specific responsibilities to the defined 'Transporter.' Changing the term introduces ambiguity regarding who is responsible for delivering the Customer's Gas, creating a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which explicitly states the Transporter's obligation. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition because it uses multiple terms (Transporter and Pipeline Operator) interchangeably without properly defining Pipeline Operator, leading to conflicting obligations about who is responsible for delivering gas.

perturbation.original_text: "Transportation System" shall mean the Commencement Date Facilities and all other equipment, devices, Measurement Facilities, pipelines and other facilities owned or operated by Transporter at or downstream of the Points of Receipt, necessary to receive Customer Gas at the Points of Receipt for transport to the Points of Delivery, and all additions, modifications, alterations, replacements, extensions, or expansions made thereto by Transporter from time to time, including, without limitation, any New Facilities pursuant to Article II hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: "Transportation System" shall mean the Commencement Date Facilities and all other equipment, devices, Measurement Facilities, pipelines and other facilities owned or operated by Pipeline Company at or downstream of the Points of Receipt, necessary to receive Customer Gas at the Points of Receipt for transport to the Points of Delivery, and all additions, modifications, alterations, replacements, extensions, or expansions made thereto by Transporter from time to time, including, without limitation, any New Facilities pursuant to Article II hereof.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Transporter' is changed to 'Pipeline Company' in the definition of 'Transportation System'. This creates confusion, as the agreement repeatedly refers to 'Transporter' as the entity responsible for the Transportation System. Introducing 'Pipeline Company', without defining it, makes it unclear who owns or operates the Transportation System, potentially contradicting the responsibilities assigned to 'Transporter' throughout the agreement. This may cause issues in determining who is responsible for the upkeep and operations of the transportation system.
perturbation.contradicted_text: If the quantity of Customer Gas and all other Gas available for delivery into the Transportation System exceeds the capacity of the Transportation System at any point, then Transporter shall interrupt or curtail receipts of Customer Gas with respect to the affected point(s) only in accordance with the following: (a) First, Transporter shall curtail all Interruptible Service Gas prior to curtailing Firm Service Gas. In the event Transporter curtails some, but not all, Interruptible Service Gas on a particular Day, Transporter shall allocate the capacity of the Transportation System available for Interruptible Service Gas at the affected points on a pro rata basis based upon Customer's last confirmed nomination of Interruptible Service Gas hereunder and the last confirmed nominations of Interruptible Service Gas from all other shippers on the Transportation System prior to the event causing the curtailment. (b) Second, if additional curtailments are required beyond those described in the immediately preceding clause (a), Transporter shall curtail Firm Service Gas. In the event Transporter curtails some, but not all, Firm Service Gas on a particular Day, Transporter shall allocate the capacity of the Transportation System at the affected point(s) on a pro rata basis based upon Customer's last confirmed nomination of Firm Service Gas hereunder and the last confirmed nominations of Firm Service Gas from all other shippers on the Transportation System prior to the event causing the curtailment. Transporter shall provide Customer notice of any interruption or curtailment of the receipt of Customer Gas into the Transportation System as is reasonable under the circumstances.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Transporter' with 'Pipeline Company' in the definition of 'Transportation System'. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'Transporter' shall interrupt or curtail receipts of Customer Gas with respect to the affected point(s). By redefining the owner/operator of the 'Transportation System', it's unclear if 'Transporter' retains the responsibility for curtailment, creating a contradiction and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: In the event Transporter is required to pay or remit any such Tax or surcharge owed by Customer as a result of Customer Gas being transported on the Transportation System, Customer shall reimburse Transporter for such Tax or surcharge pursuant to Transporter's invoice for the same.
perturbation.changed_text: In the event Carrier is required to pay or remit any such Tax or surcharge owed by Customer as a result of Customer Gas being transported on the Transportation System, Customer shall reimburse Transporter for such Tax or surcharge pursuant to Transporter's invoice for the same.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Transporter' is changed to 'Carrier' in the section regarding tax reimbursement. While the invoice is still issued by 'Transporter', the obligation to pay the tax arises if the 'Carrier' pays the tax. This creates ambiguity as 'Carrier' is not a defined term, leaving uncertainty as to which entity's actions trigger the reimbursement obligation. 'Transporter' is a defined entity and clear throughout the agreement as the one performing obligations and billing. This change creates conflicting obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Transporter shall be responsible for all Taxes or surcharges levied or imposed on it by any Governmental Authority with respect to the Transportation System and Transporter's other facilities, including without limitation, Transporter's Gas gathering system, processing plants and Gas liquids pipeline system.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly states that 'Transporter' is responsible for all taxes related to transporting Customer Gas, and the customer reimburses 'Transporter'. The changed text replaces 'Transporter' with 'Carrier', but the reimbursement obligation is still tied to 'Transporter's invoice. Because 'Carrier' is not a defined term in the agreement, this introduces ambiguity and creates a strong contradiction. If 'Carrier' pays the tax (instead of Transporter), it's unclear whether the customer is obligated to reimburse because the invoice is still issued by 'Transporter' and 'Transporter' is the party originally liable for the taxes. This conflicts directly with the contradicted text, which assigns tax responsibility to the 'Transporter', making the reimbursement obligation unclear and creates uncertainty as to which entity's actions trigger the reimbursement obligation.
